Metadata.title: LivingBots — Igor Felipe
Metadata.description: Software that feels alive: games, web concepts, and technical consulting by Igor Felipe, solo developer at LivingBots.

Hero.tagline: I build software that feels alive.
Hero.sub: Games, websites, and healthier codebases — by Igor Felipe, one developer with 16 years of code and a habit of shipping in small checkpoints.

Games.heading: Games
Games.intro: Four games, each honestly labeled with where it actually stands.

shooting-stars.blurb: Face the endless war, and make the sky fall at your feet. Top-down Vampire Survivors × tower defense hybrid, targeting CrazyGames. i18n EN/pt-BR.

stack-rivals.blurb: Precision stacking game. Synthwave aesthetic, heatmap death-tracking, seeded daily challenge, 7 locales. CrazyGames SDK integrated, release pending.

bomb-arena.blurb: Gunbound-style artillery with real server-side physics — built as a technical demo for a competitive-gaming-platform client.

orbit-runner.blurb: One-thumb arcade — a dot latching onto orbiting planets. Anti-cheat architecture (replay verification), modifier-fork economy design.

arcanes.blurb: Explore the land of Hyperion and all its trials.

smplanes.blurb: Play against everyone in this frenetic game.

Games.closingLine: Earlier games were published on Google Play and have since been delisted.

Web.heading: Web
Web.intro: Concept and demo work: sites built to show what I'd build for you.

acai-joca.name: Açaí do Joca Jr.
acai-joca.label: Concept
acai-joca.blurb: One-scroll landing concept for a local açaí brand, built for B2B lead capture.

gaming-platform.name: Competitive gaming platform (anonymized)
gaming-platform.label: Active engagement
gaming-platform.blurb: Ongoing technical advisory: engagement proposal tiers and backend architecture analysis (SpacetimeDB vs. Nakama), with Bomb Arena as a capability demo.

Consulting.heading: Consulting
Consulting.intro: Technical guidance for founders and vibe coders building with AI — development workflow, security, architecture.

see.label: See
see.desc: A scanner sweep across the codebase, exposing every bug already there.

sense.label: Sense
sense.desc: Warning pulses at the places where failures are about to happen.

fix.label: Fix
fix.desc: Worker bots swarm in and repair what's broken.

strengthen.label: Strengthen
strengthen.desc: A lattice locks in around the healed code, so it stays that way.

Factory.heading: The Factory
Factory.intro: GitHub Issues → Inngest workflows → Claude Code running headless → deterministic CI gates → per-ticket release branches, two-stage regression, trusted-ref adapter pattern so untrusted PR branches can't escalate permissions.
Factory.framing: I run my own software factory — I write the tickets, the pipeline implements.
Factory.gatesHeading: The gates every ticket has to clear

Contact.heading: Contact
Contact.copy: Tell me what you're building — I'll tell you honestly whether I can help and what the first checkpoint looks like.
